fact: The first official Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground.
Sachin Tendulkar is the highest run scorer in international cricket.
The ICC Cricket World Cup is held every four years.
India won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup in 1983 under Kapil Dev.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930 in Uruguay.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup five times.
A standard football match lasts 90 minutes.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's top club football competition.
The Thomas Cup is the world championship for men's badminton teams.
India won its first Thomas Cup title in 2022.
Badminton became an Olympic sport in 1992.
PV Sindhu won India's first Olympic silver medal in badminton.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.
There are four Grand Slam tournaments each year.
The Australian Open is played on hard courts.
Rafael Nadal has won multiple French Open titles.
Basketball was invented by James Naismith in 1891.
An NBA game consists of four quarters.
The basket is 10 feet above the ground.
The NBA is the world's premier professional basketball league.
Field hockey is India's national game according to popular belief, although it has no officially declared national sport.
India has won multiple Olympic gold medals in field hockey.
A hockey team has eleven players on the field.
The Hockey World Cup is organized by the International Hockey Federation.
Volleyball was invented by William G. Morgan in 1895.
A volleyball team has six players on the court.
A match is generally played as the best of five sets.
Volleyball became an Olympic sport in 1964.
Table tennis is also known as ping pong.
A table tennis game is usually played to 11 points.
The International Table Tennis Federation governs the sport.
Table tennis became an Olympic sport in 1988.
